title: What makes paper accept watercolour — surface and sizing
sources: Dard Hunter, Old Papermaking (1923, public domain) — sizing and surface treatment for writing and art papers
notes: Why watercolour paper works the way it does: the role of internal (engine) sizing vs surface sizing, cold-press (NOT) texture compared to hot-press smooth, the 100% cotton content and how it holds water, gsm and how it affects buckling. Useful for papermakers making sheets intended for watercolour or calligraphy use. Cross-links to surface-sizing-for-watercolour.
